title: Arapatiella psilophylla
taxonomy: Arapatiella psilophylla is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae. It is native to South America and is a part of the Araipa genus. Arapatiella psilophylla is a vine that grows in the tropical regions of the continent. The plant's classification places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. Further classification includes the order Lamiales and the family Bignoniaceae.

intro: Arapatiella psilophylla, also known as Pilocarpus psilophyllus, is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The health benefits of Arapatiella psilophylla include its potential to lower blood pressure, treat glaucoma, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include pilocarpine, pilocarpin, and other alkaloids that contribute to its medicinal properties. Arapatiella psilophylla is typically used to make herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and infusions, which are often used to treat eye and cardiovascular conditions.
